name: sufficit-vscode-symposium
description: Symposium (συμπόσιον) — host and conduct dialogue sessions with multiple AI agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, GitHub Copilot CLI) side by side inside VS Code.

commands.title: Symposium: New Persistent Session (tmux, survives close)
Symposium: List VS Code LM Tools (diagnostics)

symposium.commit.preset.markdownDescription: Sufficit AI **preset** used to generate Source Control commit messages. Must match a preset name from the gateway's `/api/tags`; leave as-is to auto-pick a VS Code preset.

symposium.commit.origin.markdownDescription: Origin of the Sufficit AI service that serves the VS Code Ollama gateway used for commit-message generation.

symposium.root.description: Local root for vendor-neutral agent knowledge (agents/skills/tools/instructions, sync state). Empty uses ~/.symposium.

symposium.turnSilenceMinutes.description: Minutes of no activity from the agent (no streamed text/tool events) before a turn is force-ended with a retryable error, so a stalled tool or dropped connection can't pin the session as "working" forever. Applies to all backends (Claude, Codex, Copilot, Sufficit AI/custom). Raise it for scenarios with long silent tool calls (e.g. slow test suites); set to 0 to disable the watchdog entirely.

symposium.hub.url.description: Base URL of the sufficit-ai memory/vault hub (e.g. https://ai.sufficit.com.br). Empty disables sync; agents still work offline from local files.

symposium.identity.url.description: Sufficit Identity (Duende) issuer URL for login.

symposium.identity.clientId.description: OAuth client id registered in Sufficit Identity for Symposium (public client with the device_code grant). Defaults to the standard Symposium client; override only for a custom identity tenant.

symposium.hub.token.description: Bearer token for the sufficit-ai hub REST API.

symposium.openai.maxToolHops.description: Max tool round-trips an API backend (Sufficit AI / OpenAI-compatible) may run in a single turn before pausing for 'continue'. Ignored in autonomous mode (presence Away = no limit).

symposium.openai.noProgressStop.description: Anti-loop: stop the turn after this many consecutive tool steps with no assistant reply (the agent isn't converging). 0 = unlimited (never auto-stop on silence). Applies even in autonomous mode.

symposium.openai.autoCompactAt.description: Auto-compact the conversation when a request reaches this fraction (0–1) of the model's context window: older turns are summarized into one note (full transcript stays in the lossless ledger; tool outputs recoverable via read_session) so the live context shrinks. 0 disables auto-compaction (manual /compact still works).

symposium.openai.autoCompactOnTasksCompl.description: Auto-compact the conversation the moment the last pending session task is completed (task_complete/TaskUpdate reports zero remaining) — a natural end-of-work boundary, independent of the context-window percentage above.

symposium.openai.maxHistoryMessages.description: Max recent conversation messages sent to OpenAI-compatible backends per request. System/developer prompts are preserved separately. Lower this when long tool-heavy sessions hit provider context limits; 0 disables local trimming.

enumDescriptions: Never mention elapsed time — the model is never told about real-world gaps between messages.
Note it once the gap since your last message is at least 5 minutes (default).
Note it once the gap since your last message is at least 30 minutes.
Note it once the gap since your last message is at least 2 hours.
Note it once the gap since your last message is at least 12 hours.
symposium.openai.timeGapNotice.description: Tell the model when it's resuming after a large real-world gap (e.g. you're back a day later) so it doesn't assume the conversation is continuous. A compact note (e.g. '~14h since your last message') is injected only when the gap meets this threshold.

enumDescriptions: No approval needed for any activity (default).
Approval needed only for destructive actions (e.g. shell commands); writes run immediately.
Approval needed for every write action, not just destructive ones.
Read-only planning — the only write allowed is creating a new *.md document.
symposium.openai.permissionMode.description: Default permission mode for this session's composer (same 4 modes shared by every Symposium adapter; changeable per session from the composer's permission picker).

enumDescriptions: Wait for the command to finish and show only the tool result.
Stream stdout/stderr into the chat while the command runs.
Open a visible VS Code terminal for the command, and still return captured output to the model.
symposium.openai.shellExecution.description: How the native Sufficit AI/OpenAI shell tool (Ran) is surfaced to the user.

symposium.hub.contextId.description: Optional memory context (tenant boundary) sent as X-MEMORY-CONTEXT-ID.

symposium.hub.source.description: Source system id sent as X-MEMORY-SOURCE-ID (identifies this machine/system in shared memory).

symposium.chat.sessionsSide.description: Side of the sessions list inside the chat surface. 'auto' follows the dock side (sessions on the outer edge: right when docked right, left when docked left).

symposium.chat.openIn.description: Where sessions open: as a full editor panel (default, like the built-in chat sessions viewer) or in the Symposium sidebar Chat view.

enumDescriptions: Don't expose VS Code Language Model Tools to the native AI backend. Commands continue through Symposium's own shell tool.
Expose compatible registered VS Code Language Model Tools. Terminal, task and test tools are excluded because VS Code only permits them from its own Chat request.
symposium.lmTools.description: Which compatible VS Code Language Model Tools (vscode.lm.tools) the native 'Sufficit AI' backend may call alongside its own shell/filesystem tools. Use Command execution below for terminal commands.

symposium.lmToolsBlocklist.description: Extra VS Code Language Model Tools to never bridge to the native 'Sufficit AI' backend (substring match on the tool name). Copilot's own memory tools (copilot_memory and *memory* tools, which write to Copilot's store — NOT the Sufficit memory) are always blocked regardless of this list.

symposium.subagents.timeoutMs.description: How long (ms) a foreground spawn_agent waits for the subagent to finish before returning the partial result and leaving it running in the background. Default 300000 (5 min).

enumDescriptions: Web Speech in the browser (code-server); local whisper.cpp on desktop.
Browser Web Speech API only (works in code-server, not VS Code desktop).
whisper.cpp via the whisper-cli binary (offline, CPU, multilingual).
faster-whisper via whisper-ctranslate2 (the tool downloads its own models).
Vosk via vosk-transcriber (pip install vosk; lightweight, streaming).
symposium.voice.engine.description: Speech-to-text engine for voice input. 'auto' uses the browser engine where available and falls back to whisper.cpp locally.

symposium.voice.language.description: Recognition language (BCP-47, e.g. pt-BR, en-US). Local engines use the language part (pt, en).

symposium.voice.modelsDir.description: Directory for downloaded STT models. Empty uses the extension global storage.

symposium.voice.ffmpegPath.description: Path to ffmpeg, used to convert captured audio to 16 kHz mono WAV. Empty uses 'ffmpeg' on PATH.

symposium.voice.continuous.description: Web Speech: keep listening across pauses instead of stopping after one phrase.

symposium.voice.interimResults.description: Web Speech: show interim (non-final) transcription while speaking.

symposium.voice.dotsAnimation.description: Show an animated dots indicator in the input while recording.

symposium.voice.soundFeedback.description: Play short start/stop tones when recording begins and ends.

symposium.voice.whisper.binaryPath.description: Path to the whisper.cpp 'whisper-cli' binary. Empty uses 'whisper-cli' on PATH.

symposium.voice.whisper.model.description: whisper.cpp model id (tiny, base, small, medium, large-v3-turbo, ...). Download it from Settings → Voice.

symposium.voice.whisper.translate.description: whisper.cpp: translate the speech to English instead of transcribing in the source language.

symposium.voice.whisper.beamSize.description: whisper.cpp: beam search size (higher = more accurate, slower).

symposium.voice.whisper.initialPrompt.description: whisper.cpp: optional initial prompt to bias vocabulary/spelling.

symposium.voice.fasterWhisper.binaryPath.description: Path to the 'whisper-ctranslate2' binary (faster-whisper). Empty uses it from PATH.

symposium.voice.fasterWhisper.model.description: faster-whisper model name (tiny, base, small, medium, large-v3). The tool downloads it on first use.

symposium.voice.fasterWhisper.computeTyp.description: faster-whisper compute precision (int8 = fastest/CPU, float16 = GPU).

symposium.voice.fasterWhisper.vad.description: faster-whisper: enable voice-activity-detection filtering of silence.

symposium.voice.vosk.binaryPath.description: Path to the 'vosk-transcriber' binary. Empty uses it from PATH.

symposium.voice.vosk.model.description: Vosk model id. Download it from Settings → Voice.

symposium.bridge.enabled.description: Enable the remote control bridge (HTTP + SSE) that re-publishes the Symposium public API for remote commands and chat following. Off by default.

symposium.bridge.host.description: Bind address for the remote bridge. Keep localhost unless fronted by an authenticated tunnel.

symposium.bridge.port.description: TCP port for the remote bridge.

symposium.bridge.token.description: Bearer token required by the remote bridge. Empty generates an ephemeral token logged to the Symposium output channel.

symposium.bridge.pwa.description: Serve the installable remote PWA at /pwa. Every data endpoint remains token-gated.

symposium.bridge.allowedRoots.description: Absolute directories a remotely-created session may use as its working directory. A remote spawn is code execution, so it is confined to these roots. Empty falls back to the open workspace folders; with no workspace open, remote session creation is refused.

symposium.bridge.sessionPermission.description: Permission mode forced onto every session created via the remote bridge. bypassPermissions / never are deliberately not offered — a remote request must never run the agent's shell/edits unattended without approval.

symposium.bridge.allowedLmTools.description: Exact VS Code Language Model tool names a remote caller may invoke via /vscode/lmtool. Empty = none (the default), because LM tools can include terminal execution.

symposium.bridge.allowExecutableOverride.description: Allow a remote caller to rewrite a backend's executable path. Off by default — it is a clean remote code-execution primitive.

symposium.bridge.allowVaultResolve.description: Allow a remote caller to read vault secret values via /vault/resolve. Off by default.

symposium.bridge.allowedHosts.description: Accepted HTTP Host header values (e.g. your-node.ts.net), for anti DNS-rebinding. Loopback is always accepted. Empty cannot enforce (the tunnel hostname is unknown) and logs a warning; set it once you have a stable hostname.

symposium.claude.executable.description: Path to the Claude Code CLI executable.

symposium.claude.model.description: Model passed to claude --model. Empty uses the CLI default.

enumDescriptions: No approval needed for any activity (default). Maps to claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions.
Not yet enforced for the Claude CLI — runs as admin until Claude's hook system is wired up for this.
Not yet enforced for the Claude CLI — runs as admin until Claude's hook system is wired up for this.
Read-only planning. Maps to claude --permission-mode plan.
Legacy value: Claude's own acceptEdits flag, passed through as-is.
Legacy value: Claude's own bypassPermissions flag, passed through as-is.
Legacy value: claude's built-in default (interactive prompts) — not usable headlessly.
symposium.claude.permissionMode.description: Default permission mode for this session's composer (same 4 modes shared by every Symposium adapter; changeable per session from the composer's permission picker).

symposium.claude.env.description: Extra environment variables for the Claude CLI process (e.g. ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN for a gateway).

symposium.claude.playwright.description: Give Claude the Playwright MCP browser tools (assisted navigation: open pages, click, type, snapshot, screenshot) via @playwright/mcp. Requires npx; first use downloads the browser.

symposium.claude.mcpServers.description: Extra MCP servers exposed to Claude, merged into --mcp-config. Shape: { "name": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "pkg"] } }.

symposium.claude.reasoning.description: Default reasoning/thinking effort for Claude terminal sessions (claude --effort). 'default' omits the flag.

symposium.codex.executable.description: Path to the Codex CLI executable.

symposium.codex.model.description: Model passed to codex exec --model. Empty uses the CLI default.

enumDescriptions: No approval needed for any activity (default). Runs with --sandbox danger-full-access.
Not yet enforced for the Codex CLI — runs as admin until Codex's approval protocol is wired up for this.
Not yet enforced for the Codex CLI — runs as admin until Codex's approval protocol is wired up for this.
Read-only planning. Runs with --sandbox read-only.
Legacy value: Codex's own untrusted approval_policy, passed through as-is (uses symposium.codex.sandboxMode).
Legacy value: Codex's own on-failure approval_policy, passed through as-is (uses symposium.codex.sandboxMode).
Legacy value: Codex's own on-request approval_policy, passed through as-is (uses symposium.codex.sandboxMode).
Legacy value: Codex's own never approval_policy, passed through as-is (uses symposium.codex.sandboxMode).
Legacy alias for admin.
symposium.codex.approvalPolicy.description: Default permission mode for this session's composer (same 4 modes shared by every Symposium adapter; changeable per session from the composer's permission picker).

symposium.codex.sandboxMode.description: Sandbox mode for Codex terminal sessions using a legacy approvalPolicy value (untrusted/on-failure/on-request/never). Ignored by the unified admin/manager/user/plan modes, which set their own sandbox.

symposium.codex.playwright.description: Give Codex the Playwright MCP browser tools (assisted navigation) via @playwright/mcp. Requires npx; first use downloads the browser.

symposium.codex.mcpServers.description: Extra MCP servers for Codex ({ "name": { "command": "npx", "args": [...] } }), added as -c mcp_servers overrides.

symposium.codex.reasoning.description: Default reasoning effort for Codex terminal sessions (codex -c model_reasoning_effort). 'default' omits it.

symposium.copilot.executable.description: Path to the GitHub Copilot CLI executable.

symposium.copilot.model.description: Model passed to copilot --model. Empty uses the CLI default.

symposium.copilot.playwright.description: Give Copilot the Playwright MCP browser tools via @playwright/mcp (--mcp-config). Experimental: depends on the Copilot CLI's MCP support.

symposium.copilot.reasoning.description: Default reasoning effort for Copilot terminal sessions (copilot --reasoning-effort). 'default' omits it.

symposium.adapters.markdownDescription: Extra OpenAI-compatible adapters. Each entry: `{ name, baseUrl, headers, apiKey, model, models, supportsDeveloperRole }`. `id` is auto-generated (GUID) on save if omitted — keeps sessions stable when you rename. Leave `models` empty to auto-discover from `GET <baseUrl>/models`. Set `supportsDeveloperRole: true` only for gateways that truly accept the OpenAI `developer` role; otherwise developer prompts are downgraded to `system`. Edit via 'Symposium: Edit Adapters (JSON)'.

id.description: Stable backend key. Auto-generated (GUID, no hyphens) on save if omitted; set manually in any format if you prefer.

name.description: Display name shown in the UI.

apiKey.description: Sent as Authorization: Bearer <key> when no Authorization header is set.

supportsDeveloperRole.description: Whether this gateway supports the OpenAI `developer` role. False downgrades developer prompts to `system`.

symposium.openai.api.description: Request type for the built-in OpenAI adapter: 'chat' (/chat/completions) or 'responses' (/responses).

symposium.openai.baseUrl.description: Base URL of an OpenAI-compatible API. Defaults to the Sufficit AI gateway; override only to point the built-in backend at another OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

symposium.openai.apiKey.description: API key sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <key>' when no Authorization header is set in symposium.openai.headers.

symposium.openai.headers.description: Custom HTTP headers for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g. Authorization, x-api-key, gateway routing for Sufficit AI).

symposium.openai.model.description: Default model id. Empty uses the first of symposium.openai.models.

symposium.openai.models.description: Model ids offered in the picker (the Sufficit AI catalog).

symposium.openai.reasoning.description: Default reasoning_effort for models that support it. 'default' omits the parameter.

symposium.chat.preferredLanguage.description: Preferred language for AI responses (e.g. 'pt-br', 'es', 'zh-cn'). Leave empty to use VS Code's display language. Set to 'en' to disable the preference hint entirely.

symposium.chat.systemInstruction.description: Optional manual system instruction prepended to every new conversation's system prompt. Use it to give all agents persistent guidance.

symposium.chat.memoryInstruction.default: Before asking the user for context you lack (infrastructure, servers, credentials, config, prior decisions) or saying you don't know, search Sufficit shared memory first: memory_search → memory_get_observations. Only ask the user if that comes up empty.
symposium.chat.memoryInstruction.description: Sufficit memory search hint injected into the system prompt for logged-in users. Clear the field to disable injection entirely.

enumDescriptions: Queue the message and send it after the current turn (FIFO).
Interrupt the running turn and send immediately.
symposium.chat.whenBusy.description: What sending does WHILE a turn is running. When the agent is idle it always sends immediately, so this only applies when it's busy. Overridable per message with the dropdown next to the send button.

symposium.compression.defaultPresetId.description: ID do preset de compressão padrão para novas seções de chat.

symposium.compression.presets.description: Configuração customizada de presets de compressão (JSON array de CompressionPreset). Use a tela de configurações para editar.

symposium.compression.showSelector.description: Mostra seletor de compressão na interface de chat.

symposium.compression.showDiagnostics.description: Mostra diagnóstico de compressão na barra de status.

scripts.test: npm run compile:test && node --test out/test/*.test.js && npm run check:webview && npm run check:configscript && npm run check:size
scripts.compile: npm run clean && tsc -p ./ && npm run build:webview && npm run build:pwa && npm run bundle
scripts.compile:test: npm run clean && tsc -p ./ && npm run build:webview
scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run compile && npm run check:webview
